perturbation.original_text: This distributorship will be independent of the endorsement terms of the agreement and will remain on going as long as the CELEBRITY maintains the annual membership fee.
perturbation.changed_text: This distributorship will be independent of the endorsement terms of the agreement and will remain on going as long as the CELEBRITY maintains the monthly membership dues.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mentions 'annual membership fee,' but the modified text refers to 'monthly membership dues' of the distributorship. This creates confusion and in-text contradiction, as it is unclear how frequently the fees must be paid or if the payment type has changed, annual or monthly. This contradiction directly affects the enforceability of the distributorship terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The CELEBRITY will assist in getting a distributorship started through his contacts and fan mail. The CELEBRITY can designate the party placed in the distributorship direct to the Company. This distributorship will be independent of the endorsement terms of the agreement and will remain on going as long as the CELEBRITY maintains the monthly membership dues.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses "annual membership fee", while the changed text refers to "monthly membership dues". The contradicted text repeats the "monthly membership dues". This directly creates conflicting obligations regarding the frequency of payment. The contradiction lies in whether the payment is annual or monthly, impacting the terms of the distributorship and creating a strong in-text contradiction, thus causing confusion and potential disputes. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Jack Zufelt will coordinate all efforts and work direct with the CELEBRITY and or the designee.
perturbation.changed_text: Jack Zufelt will coordinate all efforts and work direct with the Performer and or the designee.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'CELEBRITY' is changed to 'Performer' in section 4(i). While Bruce Jenner is referred to as CELEBRITY in the opening of the agreement, interchanging it with 'Performer' creates ambiguity. This is because the contract does not specify if CELEBRITY and Performer are interchangable. This ambiguity creates uncertainty about who Jack Zufelt will coordinate with and creates an in-text contradiction that affects the obligations stated in the agreement that affects multiple sections.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The COMPANY will flag the CELEBRITY center at level six for a six month period. COMPANY will provide the necessary time to work with the business on products, strategies and opportunity. Jack Zufelt will coordinate all efforts and work direct with the Performer and or the designee.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified 'CELEBRITY', which is a defined term (Bruce Jenner). By changing this to 'Performer' without defining 'Performer', the contract introduces ambiguity as to whom Jack Zufelt should coordinate with. The contradicted text references 'CELEBRITY' directly, and the change to 'Performer' creates uncertainty because it is not specified if the terms are interchangeable. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' as the contract uses multiple terms without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations and thus a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Accordingly, CELEBRITY will be responsible for payment of all federal, state, and local taxes on compensation paid under this Agreement, including income and social security taxes, unemployment insurance, and any other taxes due relative to Distributor's Personnel and any and all business license fees as may be required.
perturbation.changed_text: Accordingly, the Artist will be responsible for payment of all federal, state, and local taxes on compensation paid under this Agreement, including income and social security taxes, unemployment insurance, and any other taxes due relative to Distributor's Personnel and any and all business license fees as may be required.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'CELEBRITY' to 'Artist' in section 5 introduces another ambiguity. The agreement initially defines Bruce Jenner as 'CELEBRITY', but the sudden use of 'Artist' without defining it as interchangeable with 'CELEBRITY' or even 'Performer' (as introduced in the prior perturbation) creates confusion about who is responsible for paying taxes. The original intent was to assign this responsibility to Bruce Jenner (the CELEBRITY), but this has been blurred. This creates an in-text contradiction that affects the obligations stated in the agreement that affects multiple sections.
perturbation.contradicted_text: INDEPENDENT CONTRACTOR CELEBRITY and CELEBRITY Personnel will act as an independent contractor in the performance of its duties under this Agreement. Accordingly, the Artist will be responsible for payment of all federal, state, and local taxes on compensation paid under this Agreement, including income and social security taxes, unemployment insurance, and any other taxes due relative to Distributor's Personnel and any and all business license fees as may be required. This Agreement neither expressly nor impliedly creates a --- relationship of principal and agent, or employee and employer, between Distributor's Personnel and the COMPANY. Neither CELEBRITY nor CELEBRITY Personnel are authorized to enter into any agreements on behalf of the COMPANY. The COMPANY expressly retains the right to approve, in its sole discretion, each Asset Opportunity or Business Opportunity introduced by CELEBRITY, and to make all final decisions with respect to effecting a transaction on any Business Opportunity.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'CELEBRITY' with 'Artist' regarding tax responsibilities. The contradicted text repeats the tax obligation, using 'Artist' in the same place where 'CELEBRITY' was expected based on the agreement's initial definition. Since 'Artist' isn't explicitly defined as interchangeable with 'CELEBRITY', or even 'Performer', this creates ambiguity and contradiction about who is responsible for tax payments, which directly impacts the financial obligations detailed in the agreement, violating the misaligned terminology definition and creating an in-text contradiction.
